text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members frequently integrate their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and a series of tests. These automated builds compile the code and run unit and integration tests to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. A CI pipeline orchestrates this entire process, from code commit to test execution. By running tests on every change, developers receive immediate feedback, allowing them to fix bugs quickly. This approach improves code quality and speeds up development.
